text: Why does the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV (BKdV) system tend to relax locally to a compound-soliton structure from generic ICs? What is the basin of attraction? In what precise sense is the local compound soliton equivalent to a Gardner soliton, and how does the dynamical equivalence break down globally?

hypothesis_tested: H1 local-lock: smooth-localized v-ICs drive u toward v^2/2 (or some linear function of v^2) LOCALLY inside the v-peak support, producing a coherent (u,v) bound structure; H_alt_subthreshold: low-amplitude IC fails to form compound; H_alt_two_pulse: two-pulse IC produces two compound peaks not merged.
design: Run full BKdV with the validated solver across four ICs at fixed (Nx=256, L=30, dt=1e-4, T=10): IC-A v0=sech^2(x+5) A=1.0; IC-B v0=Gaussian A=1.0; IC-C v0=Gaussian A=0.2 (sub-threshold control); IC-D two-pulse sech^2. All u0=0; nu=5e-2 on u (per BKdV-S6 deep). Track per-step lock_corr(u, v^2/2) inside in-peak mask (|v|>0.2*v_max), m_rms_in, m_rms_out, peak centroid, snapshots at t={0,1,2,4,6,8,10}.
method: Fourier pseudospectral (Nx=256), 2/3 dealias on every nonlinear product, classical RK4, dt=1e-4, periodic L=30, linear viscosity nu=5e-2 on u-equation (per BKdV-S6 deep).
cites_bank: BKdV-S1 (positive, deep): Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + RK4 dt=2e-4 stable at amp 3.0 - used as solver foundation (we tighten dt to 1e-4 for safety in coupled regime)

BKdV-S6 (negative, deep depth=3): smoothed-bore u IC requires linear viscosity nu*u_xx with nu~5e-2 to bound Burgers self-flux; transferred even with u0=0 because the v^2 coupling can develop u-gradients dynamically
BKdV-S7 (positive): Gardner-only baseline at A=1.5 propagates cleanly with mass <1e-12, sup ~ 1.5 - establishes Gardner side is numerically inert at moderate A

BKdV-S7 (positive): full BKdV from m_0=0 sech^2 IC at A=1.5 shows ||m||_L2 grows to 2.55 by T=10, v fragments to 8 peaks - confirms m=0 manifold is repelling; our E1 uses smaller A=1.0 to probe the coherent regime BKdV-S7 missed

BKdV-S3 (positive: positive-frame deep synthesis embedded in our design): smooth-localized IC families (sech^2, Gauss, two-pulse) form a coherent basin; broadband / sub-threshold should NOT - IC-C control and lift to E2 broadband
BKdV-S2 (positive, deep): C1=int u dx, C2=int v dx are machine-precision conserved by divergence form - used as numerical sanity check (verified during runs)
rejects_bank: kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs (negative): central FD blows up - rejected because we use spectral, not FD
kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup (negative): IFRK4 overflows - rejected, we use classical RK4 not IFRK4
kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts (negative): no-dealiasing inflates peak count - rejected, our solver applies 2/3 dealias on every product (BKdV-S1 lesson)

kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-periodic-longTime-contamination (negative): T=10 on periodic domain wraps - only marginally relevant; observed compound centroid at +9.0 by T=10 has not wrapped past L/2=15, so within safe window

kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup (negative): explicit RK4 v_xxx needs dt~dx^3 - rejected, BKdV-S1 already showed Fourier+2/3-dealias raises the RK4 dispersion CFL to ~4.94e-4, and our dt=1e-4 sits well inside
kb-gardner-G4-IMEX-CN-amplitudeCFL-blowup (negative): at A=3 IMEX-CN blows up - rejected, we keep A<=1 in E1 main runs
kb-shallowWater-* (negative): shallow-water specific - rejected as not directly applicable to BKdV's (u, v) system

kb-gardner-sech2IC-not-exact-soliton (negative): using KdV sech^2 IC for Gardner radiates - rejected as our v-equation is full BKdV, not isolated Gardner; sech^2 is a natural probe of the BKdV compound basin, not a claim of exactness
bank_use_rationale: Positive entries fix the solver stack (BKdV-S1, BKdV-S6 nu=5e-2, BKdV-S7 Gardner baseline). Negative entries about other numerical pitfalls are inapplicable because the prompt mandates the validated Fourier+2/3-dealias+RK4 stack; we acknowledge them and explain why they don't bite. BKdV-S3/S5/S7 deep entries directly motivate IC choice and the mechanism question.

supports_hypothesis: H1 (linear-lock in-peak) but FALSIFIES the strict m=0 / u=v^2/2 reading. The local linear correlation between u and v^2/2 inside peak support is strong (R^2 ~ 0.94 for sech^2), but the slope alpha ~ 12 is NOT 1; in-peak m_rms is actually larger than out-of-peak m_rms in absolute terms, refuting the picture 'm=0 inside peak, nonzero outside'. Sub-threshold (A=0.2) Gaussian disperses (c~0); single-pulse and two-pulse smooth ICs all produce a coherent moving compound with phase speed c ~ 1.4 essentially independent of IC details.
rationale: F1 forces a refinement: 'compound soliton with m~0 inside support' is too crude. The empirical lock is LINEAR (u and v^2/2 vary together in-peak) but the slope is amplitude/profile-dependent. The strict m=0 picture from the prompt's anchoring is falsified by direct measurement. F1 also shows the basin includes localized seeds (sech^2, Gauss, two-pulse) and excludes sub-threshold seeds - consistent with BKdV-S3 deep. Next round must distinguish what the LOCAL algebraic structure actually is and confirm the basin boundaries on independent axes (broadband, bore-driven).

next_step: E2: (a) co-evolve a Gardner equation as companion to BKdV from the same v_0 to directly measure ||v_BKdV - v_Gardner|| and compare phase speeds (test global Gardner equivalence); (b) test broadband low-amplitude IC v0=0.4(cos(2pi x/L)+0.5 cos(4pi x/L)) to probe basin on the spectral-shape axis (BKdV-S3 deep); (c) test bore-driven u IC (u0=0.6*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2) + sech v IC to check whether a Burgers bore disrupts in-peak lock (BKdV-S6 deep). Also log alpha(t) trace to see whether the slope alpha is constant or drifting.
rationale: F1 left two questions: (i) what is the relation of the compound to a Gardner soliton, and (ii) does the basin really exclude broadband/bore-perturbed ICs? E2 attacks both with a direct Gardner companion (i) and two basin-boundary probes (ii).

hypothesis_tested: H_alpha (m=0): u = v^2/2 globally; H_beta (rescaled-Gardner): u = alpha(t) v^2/2 + beta(t) in-peak with alpha not 1, and an effective Gardner with rescaled cubic governs v in-peak; H_gamma (global Gardner equivalence): v_BKdV ~ v_Gardner over t in [0,10]; H_basin_spectral: broadband ICs fail to form compound; H_basin_bore: bore-u IC disrupts in-peak lock.
design: Three sub-runs: E2a sech^2 A=1.0 with co-evolved Gardner companion (same v_0); E2b broadband low-amplitude v0=0.4(cos(2pi x/L)+0.5 cos(4pi x/L)) u0=0; E2c bore u0=0.6*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2 + sech v0=1.0 sech^2(x+5). Same solver as E1. Per-step record: alpha(t), beta(t), R^2(t) of linear u-vs-v^2/2 fit in peak support; ||v_BKdV-v_Gardner||_L2; centroid for phase speed.
method: Fourier pseudospectral, 2/3 dealias, classical RK4, dt=1e-4, nu=5e-2 on u; Gardner companion via dealiased v_t = -6vv_x - (3/2) v^2 v_x - v_xxx with RK4 dt=1e-4.
cites_bank: BKdV-S5 (negative, deep depth=3): m_t|_{m=0} = (v-1)(6 v v_x + v_xxx) - directly explains why m=0 cannot be invariant when peak v > 1, and supports H_beta over H_alpha
BKdV-S7 (positive): Gardner-only baseline runs cleanly, used to validate the Gardner companion in E2a
BKdV-S3 (negative, deep depth=3): broadband K_sin IC blows up at A>=0.8 due to high-k cascade; we keep A=0.4 to stay below this wall and isolate the BASIN question from the BLOW-UP wall
BKdV-S6 (negative, deep depth=3): bore-u IC with nu=5e-2 produces TV(u) ~ 9.6 stable, but the bore physics perturbs v through -d_x(uv) - directly motivates E2c
BKdV-S7 (positive): identity ||S||_L2 = 1.77/t for the m_t source at A=1.5 - supports the prediction that Gardner companion drifts from BKdV
rejects_bank: BKdV-S2 (negative, deep): hunt for BKdV Hamiltonian - orthogonal to E2's mechanism question (E2 is about lock structure, not conservation laws); the BKdV-S2 conclusion that quadratic energies are NOT invariants supports interpreting any drift in m_L2 as physical, not numerical
kb-gardner-G4-IMEX-CN-amplitudeCFL-blowup (negative): A=3 IMEX-CN blowup - rejected because E2 uses A<=1 and RK4, not IMEX-CN
kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-* (negative): excessive numerical diffusion - rejected, nu=5e-2 is a calibrated physical viscosity for the bore, not numerical LxF

BKdV-S4 (negative, deep): hyperviscosity at Nx=256 reshapes the attractor toward smooth-locked state - rejected because we use linear viscosity on u only and at level nu=5e-2 calibrated by BKdV-S6 with TV(u)~9.6, not strong HV on v
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S5/S7 directly predict that H_gamma is wrong, so E2a is a confirmation/quantification, not a discovery. BKdV-S3/S6 motivate the basin probes (E2b/E2c). BKdV-S4 strong-HV warning is acknowledged but not relevant because we use moderate nu only on u (no HV on v at all).

supports_hypothesis: Supports H_beta (linear in-peak lock with slope alpha!=1, rescaled-Gardner reading) and falsifies H_alpha and H_gamma. The Gardner companion drifts to ||L2 diff|| > 1 by T=10 with c_Gardner=1.93 versus c_BKdV=1.45 - Gardner is NOT the global solution. Strongly supports the basin hypothesis: broadband and bore-driven ICs fail to develop in-peak lock (R^2 < 0.1), while sech^2 IC reaches R^2=0.94.
rationale: F2 establishes: (a) The local compound is a LINEAR-rescaled lock u = alpha(t) v^2/2 + beta(t) in-peak with alpha drifting from 0 (at t=0) to ~12 at T=10 and R^2 climbing from 0 to 0.94 - emergence of the compound IS the relaxation of alpha to a near-constant value. (b) The compound is NOT a Gardner soliton: at the same initial v_0, the Gardner equation maintains v_max ~ 0.85 (clean soliton) while BKdV decays to v_max ~ 0.36 with broader support, faster spreading, and different phase speed. The 'Gardner equivalence' reading of the compound is FALSIFIED on amplitude, speed, and L^2 distance. (c) Broadband and bore-driven ICs are OUTSIDE the basin - they fail to form a coherent locked structure. This sharpens H_beta but raises a deeper question: WHAT is the local algebraic relation if not m=0 and not a clean Gardner soliton?

next_step: E3: (a) derive the LOCAL traveling-wave ansatz of BKdV by integrating the steady-state equations u(x-ct), v(x-ct) for localized fields - the algebraic constraint between U and V at speed c is U_-(V; c) = [(c+V) - sqrt(c^2 - 4cV + V^2)] / 3 from the quadratic identity (3/2)U^2 - U(c+V) + cV = 0. Test this against the E1 t=10 snapshot (and cross-checks). (b) Run an amplitude sweep A in {0.3, 0.6, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0} to map the basin in A, recording lock_max, lock_T, R^2, alpha - this distinguishes the lower-amplitude dispersive boundary from the upper-amplitude shattering boundary (BKdV-S5/S7).
rationale: F2 leaves open the precise local algebraic relation. The BKdV-S5/S7 negative-frame falsification of m=0 demands a *replacement* ansatz. Steady-state analysis of (u, v) traveling-wave equations gives a quadratic algebraic constraint, which we can test against numerical snapshots. The amplitude sweep maps the basin: BKdV-S3 deep suggests amplitude-scale-free retention within the sech^2 family, but BKdV-S5/S7 says (v-1) sign-flip at A=1 should mark a transition from compound to fragmenting regime.

hypothesis_tested: H_beta_TW (refined): local in-peak relation matches the traveling-wave algebraic constraint U_-(V; c) = [(c+V) - sqrt(c^2 - 4cV + V^2)] / 3 derived from the steady (u(x-ct), v(x-ct)) ansatz of BKdV; H_basin_amp: compound-formation basin in the sech^2 family is bounded both below (sub-threshold dispersion) and above (BKdV-S5/S7 shattering).
design: E3a: load E1 t=10 snapshots for IC-A, IC-B, IC-D; estimate c from centroid drift (t>=2 linear fit); compute four candidate in-peak residuals - (1) m=0 ansatz u-v^2/2, (2) linear-rescaled u-alpha v^2/2-beta, (3) TW small-V leading u ~ V - V^2/(6c), (4) TW full quadratic-branch U_-(V; c). E3b: amplitude sweep A in {0.3, 0.6, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0} on v0=A sech^2(x+5), u0=0, same solver, T=10. Track lock_max, lock_T, alpha_T, R^2_T per A.
method: Fourier pseudospectral, 2/3 dealias, classical RK4 dt=1e-4 on full BKdV with nu=5e-2 on u. Algebraic ansatz residuals computed offline from saved snapshots.
cites_bank: BKdV-S5 (negative, deep): m_t|_{m=0}=(v-1)(6 v v_x + v_xxx) and ||S||_L2 log-log slope 2.49 for A>=1 - predicts shattering above A=1, supporting our amplitude-sweep design
BKdV-S7 (positive, x3): demonstrates sech^2 family is the natural probe; A=1.5 shows fragmentation already by T=10
BKdV-S3 (positive deep): scan over sech^2 amplitudes 0.1..1.2 found amplitude-scale-free single-peak retention - our sweep extends this past A=1 to test the BKdV-S5 upper boundary
BKdV-S1 (positive): solver remains stable at A up to 3 in a v-only soliton; transfer to coupled at A=2 expected stable numerically but possibly shattering physically
rejects_bank: kb-gardner-sech2IC-not-exact-soliton: rejected because we are testing BKdV traveling-wave U_-(V; c), not asserting sech^2 is a BKdV exact soliton - in fact our F1/F2 already shows sech^2 is NOT a BKdV traveling wave, it relaxes to one
BKdV-S2 (negative, deep): the search for cubic Hamiltonian invariants is orthogonal; we use the simpler steady-state ansatz from balancing the divergence-form integrals on a localized profile

kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (positive): MUSCL is recommended for bores; rejected here because at A in [0.3, 2.0] starting from sech^2 the u-side does not develop a strong bore - TV(u) stays modest, and nu=5e-2 suffices per BKdV-S6
kb-gardner-cubicTerm-tightens-nonlinearCFL: rejected because we use explicit RK4 not IMEX-CN, and our amplitudes stay within E1's safe window dt=1e-4
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S5/S7 deep entries are the strongest signals for the upper boundary of the basin; BKdV-S3 deep gives the lower-amplitude side and motivates the amplitude scan. The traveling-wave ansatz is original derivation from the BKdV system, not in the bank - bank entries inform what NOT to do, but the mechanism ansatz itself comes from the system's equations directly.

supports_hypothesis: Supports H_basin_amp (compound basin has both a lower and upper amplitude boundary in the sech^2 family) and partially supports H_beta_TW with caveats. The TW full quadratic-branch ansatz U_-(V; c) gives R^2=0.73 for sech^2 - better than the small-V leading order (R^2=0.58) and dramatically better than m=0 (essentially R^2<<0). But the *empirical* linear-rescaled fit u = alpha v^2/2 + beta is STILL the best single descriptor (RMS=0.054 vs RMS_TW=0.115) for sech^2 - suggesting the late-time t=10 state is not yet a pure traveling wave; it's a slowly-modulated compound coexisting with O(0.2) radiation in the out-of-peak region.
rationale: F3 quantitatively falsifies the m=0 (Gardner-reduction) ansatz and identifies the local algebraic structure as ONE of: (i) a transient linear lock u ~ alpha(t) v^2/2 + beta(t) with alpha drifting and R^2 climbing to 0.94 as the compound emerges; or (ii) the small-V branch U_-(V; c) of the traveling-wave constraint - these two are not equivalent, and at t=10 the TW full ansatz explains 73% of the in-peak u-variance, indicating the compound is a relaxing approximation to a TW. The basin map shows a soft lower boundary near A~0.4 (lock_max climbs from 0.56 at A=0.3 to 0.90 at A=0.6 to 0.97 at A=1) and an upper failure near A~2 (lock_max=0.91 transiently but lock_T=0.16 - the compound forms and then shatters, exactly the (v-1) shattering predicted by BKdV-S5 deep).

next_step: wrap up - write hypothesis.md synthesizing E1/E2/E3 into a three-part answer (mechanism + basin + Gardner relation).
rationale: Three rounds spent. The mechanism question now has a substantive answer: the compound is a local relaxation toward a traveling-wave-like state where u and v^2/2 are linearly locked (with state-dependent slope alpha), and the formal equivalence to a Gardner soliton is FALSIFIED at every level we tested (m!=0 inside peak, slope alpha=12 not 1, BKdV-Gardner L^2 distance grows to 1.1, phase speeds 1.45 vs 1.93). The basin is amplitude-bounded (lower from dispersion, upper from BKdV-S5 shattering) and shape-bounded (localized vs broadband). A 4th round would refine the TW ansatz via Petviashvili iteration to construct a true BKdV traveling wave and compare directly with the relaxed numerical state.
